title: Is it possible to generate form submission links for past form items?

posts.author: jana.n.defend (Jana)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Support team,

I recently discovered the feature that adds a form submission link to items created via a form. I’ve now enabled this setting for future submissions.

I’d like to ask if there’s any way to generate these submission links retroactively for items that were submitted before this setting was turned on.

If that’s not possible, are there any recommended workarounds to preview the original form input as it was submitted?

Thank you for your help!

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,
As far as I know its not yet possible within Monday today, but there’s a great app named SuperForm that has this native functionality.

auth.monday.com

SuperForm - monday.com Marketplace

Advanced form - updating existing items, subitems and more

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jana:

ubmission link

hey @jana.n.defend where is that setting? “to create submission link to items created via a form.”

I can’t found it here:

image1918×952 91.5 KB

Is that feature available for paid plans only? and If I not wrong that link will allow user to update that very specific item right?

It almost cover all the use case then I guess, there is no need for 3rd party apps.
just curious

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, I ran into the exact same problem. Had hundreds of items already on our board and needed editable links for all of them, not just future ones.

Ended up using an app called FormFlow from the marketplace. Its Update Form feature generates pre-filled editable links for any existing item, doesnt matter when or how it was created. Saved us a ton of time vs trying to recreate everything through native forms.
